summary: The AST layer still has structural bypasses around schema renames, import-only edits, audit-chain writes, and chained assertion helper detection.

findings.title: schema_predicate_correction accepts semantic non-renames
findings.evidence: runSchemaPredicateCheck only compares paired lines up to min(len(minus), len(plus)), then isPureRename compares identifier slices only. A change like require.Equal(t, 200, got.Status) -> require.Equal(t, 201, got.Status) has identical identifiers and passes; extra added lines are also ignored.
findings.recommendation: Reject unmatched +/- lines, require at least one old_name -> new_name replacement, and compare full parsed expressions after applying only that allowed identifier rename.

findings.title: import-block leniency permits misplaced import lines
findings.evidence: When the on-disk file has no import declarations, import-block-check returns OK without checking hunk position. In that case '+    import "math"' inside a function can pass AST, and the regex fallback only sees an import-shaped line.
findings.recommendation: For files with no existing imports, validate against the patched file or allow additions only in the top-level package/import region, not anywhere in the file.

findings.title: Audit append uses non-portable sha256 command
findings.evidence: grant-test-edit-exception.sh computes prev_sha with `sha256`, while the verifier defines its own sha256 wrapper over sha256sum/shasum. On common Linux systems `sha256` is absent, so appending a second audit event can fail or produce a broken chain.
findings.recommendation: Use the same sha256sum/shasum fallback helper in the grant script and add a test that approves/appends at least two events in one cycle.

findings.title: Assertion helper extraction misses chained helper changes
findings.evidence: extractAssertionHelper returns the first CallExpr whose selector receiver is an Ident. For chained assertions such as assert.New(t).Equal(...), it records assert.New and can miss Equal -> NotEqual on the outer chained call.
findings.recommendation: Extract and compare all assertion call shapes, including selector chains whose receiver is another call expression, rather than stopping at the first Ident-receiver selector.
